verses.text: Woe is me! 'the good man is perished out of the earth'; all lie in wait for blood. A lament. The prophet grieves to find godliness almost gone.
Woe is me! 'the good man is perished out of the earth'; all lie in wait for blood. A lament. The prophet grieves to find godliness almost gone.
They do evil 'with both hands earnestly'; the best of them is a brier; the day of visitation comes. Eager wickedness. When sin is pursued with zeal, judgment is near.
They do evil 'with both hands earnestly'; the best of them is a brier; the day of visitation comes. Eager wickedness. When sin is pursued with zeal, judgment is near.
Trust not in friend or guide, for 'a man's enemies are the men of his own house.' Broken bonds. Sin so corrupts that even the closest cannot be trusted.
Trust not in friend or guide, for 'a man's enemies are the men of his own house.' Broken bonds. Sin so corrupts that even the closest cannot be trusted.
'Therefore I will look unto the LORD'; though fallen, 'I shall arise; when I sit in darkness, the LORD shall be a light unto me.' Faith in the dark. The believer waits on God when all else fails.
'Therefore I will look unto the LORD'; though fallen, 'I shall arise; when I sit in darkness, the LORD shall be a light unto me.' Faith in the dark. The believer waits on God when all else fails.
'Therefore I will look unto the LORD'; though fallen, 'I shall arise; when I sit in darkness, the LORD shall be a light unto me.' Faith in the dark. The believer waits on God when all else fails.
His enemy who scoffed shall be trodden down; the day to build her walls shall come. Reversal sure. God turns the tables on those who mocked His people.
His enemy who scoffed shall be trodden down; the day to build her walls shall come. Reversal sure. God turns the tables on those who mocked His people.
His enemy who scoffed shall be trodden down; the day to build her walls shall come. Reversal sure. God turns the tables on those who mocked His people.
His enemy who scoffed shall be trodden down; the day to build her walls shall come. Reversal sure. God turns the tables on those who mocked His people.
Feed thy people with thy rod, O LORD; as in 'the days of... Egypt,' show them wonders. A prayer for grace. The prophet pleads for God to shepherd and deliver again.
Feed thy people with thy rod, O LORD; as in 'the days of... Egypt,' show them wonders. A prayer for grace. The prophet pleads for God to shepherd and deliver again.
The nations shall see and be confounded, lay their hand on their mouth, and fear the LORD. Awe before God. The proud are silenced by His mighty works.
The nations shall see and be confounded, lay their hand on their mouth, and fear the LORD. Awe before God. The proud are silenced by His mighty works.

'Who is a God like unto thee, that pardoneth iniquity... he delighteth in mercy... thou wilt cast all their sins into the depths of the sea.' Matchless grace. The book ends marvelling at the God who forgives and forgets.
